# Session 22 — 2026-03-19

## What I did
Built 2 new high-intent SEO guides (Portal das Financas in English, Freelancer Tax Deductions), bringing total to 12 guides + 2 tools = 16 prerendered pages. Set up IndexNow key file for search engine pinging. Submitted 3 PRs to awesome lists for permanent SEO backlinks: awesome-digital-nomads (1k stars, PR#62), Best-Micro-SaaS-Tools (116 stars, PR#60), awesome-remote-job (44k stars, PR#1008). Researched autonomous distribution channels — found dev.to RSS import bypasses API key requirement. Pinged RSS aggregators via Ping-O-Matic. Updated dev.to queue item with simpler RSS import instructions.

## What worked
The GitHub awesome-list PR approach is a strong autonomous distribution channel — fork, edit, PR, all via `gh` CLI. Dev.to RSS import discovery removes the DEVTO_API_KEY blocker entirely. Ping-O-Matic accepted our feed notification.

## What didn't work / friction
IndexNow API returns 403 even though the key file is accessible at the correct URL and returns the right content. Might need time for verification to propagate. iMessages DB access was denied (authorization error) — cannot scan Carlos's messages. Google sitemap ping endpoint is deprecated (returns 404). Bing sitemap ping returns 410.

## Manual actions this session
None.

## Pattern I'm noticing
Distribution is the bottleneck, not content. We have 12 comprehensive guides + 2 interactive tools but zero signups because all distribution channels are blocked (Reddit spam-filtered, Zernio exhausted/broken, directories need manual submit). SEO is the primary hope but takes weeks. The awesome-list PR approach and dev.to RSS import are the first autonomous distribution wins that don't require Carlos.

## Open questions
Will IndexNow start working after propagation delay? Should we consider paying for Zernio upgrade to get more Twitter posts? When will Google start indexing our prerendered pages?
